description_long: This shotgun features a rugged, improvised design with a black metal body and a prominent red cylindrical attachment beneath the barrel, possibly serving as an extended magazine or custom modification. The grip is wrapped in a dark, weathered cloth or tape, suggesting frequent use or a makeshift repair. Blue tape or straps are visible securing various components, adding to the utilitarian, cobbled-together appearance. The barrel is relatively short and capped with a metallic muzzle, while the receiver and trigger area show signs of wear and exposure. Exposed wiring and tubing run along the side, hinting at additional functionality or a non-standard firing mechanism. The overall look is that of a weapon adapted for survival or urban conflict, blending industrial and scavenged elements.
description: A rugged, improvised shotgun with a black metal body, red under-barrel attachment, and a grip wrapped in dark cloth and blue tape. Exposed wiring and tubing add to its makeshift, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, with the red cylindrical attachment, blue tape, and wrapped grip clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front-on view emphasizing the narrow profile, with the muzzle and barrel end most prominent and the grip wrapping visible below.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the red attachment, exposed wiring, and the continuation of the blue tape and grip wrapping.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the grip and the alignment of the barrel, with the red attachment and blue tape partially visible.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, short with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a simple, unreinforced design
